description:
Fix code example quote style in plugin-development.mdx to match scaffolded plugin .prettierrc (singleQuote: true). The 'Understand the Plugin Class' section uses double quotes in TypeScript examples, but the scaffolded plugin's .prettierrc enforces single quotes. Developers who copy code from the guide into their scaffolded plugin will get lint errors on first run.

Known minor issues (not stories):
- `npx create-opentabs-plugin` returns 404 due to scoped package naming — inherent npm limitation, docs already use correct `npx @opentabs-dev/create-plugin` invocation
- `npx opentabs-plugin build` returns 404 when not installed locally — same scoped package naming limitation
- Both could be mitigated by publishing small public shim packages that print helpful redirect messages, but this requires a policy decision on publishing public packages
qualityChecks: cd docs && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run knip && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Fix double-quote code examples in plugin-development.mdx to use single quotes
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want the plugin development guide's code examples to use single quotes so that code I copy into my scaffolded plugin passes lint without modification.
acceptanceCriteria: The 'Understand the Plugin Class' code block (around line 68-85) uses single quotes for all string literals
The import statement uses single quotes: import { OpenTabsPlugin, type ToolDefinition } from '@opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk'
All readonly property assignments use single quotes: readonly name = 'my-app'
No other code blocks in the file are affected (they already use single quotes)
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes: File: docs/content/docs/guides/plugin-development.mdx, lines 67-85. The 'Understand the Plugin Class' section has a TypeScript code block that uses double quotes for string literals ("my-app", "0.0.1", "OpenTabs plugin for My App", "My App"). Change these to single quotes to match the .prettierrc that the scaffolder generates (singleQuote: true). Only this one code block needs changing — the rest of the file's code examples already use single quotes. Also update the import on line 68 from double quotes to single quotes.
